texto: What white middle America loathes these days are poor and poorish people, especially the kind who look and sound like they just might live in a house trailer. They will swear on a stack of Lands' End catalogs that they are not bigots, but, human nature being what it is, we are all kicking someone else's dog around, whether we admit it or not.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Joe Bageant: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “What white middle America loathes these days are poor and poorish people, especially the kind who…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, bigotry, class pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e class.
